origin.name: India
origin.investigating_authority: Local police; post-mortem at government hospital
origin.post_mortem_trigger: Routinely ordered by Indian police for unexpected deaths of foreign nationals
origin.police_noc_note: Police No Objection Certificate (NOC) required before body can be released. Standard in unexpected foreign national deaths.
origin.state_variation_note: India has 28 states and 8 union territories, each with its own procedures. Remote locations add significant time.
origin.embalming_urgency: URGENT. India's tropical climate requires immediate embalming. Quality of embalming outside major cities can vary.
origin.key_document: Indian death certificate (municipal authority) and Police NOC
origin.source: FCDO India travel advice, updated 2025; British High Commission New Delhi guidance

destinations.name: United Kingdom
special_rules: Post-mortem is routinely ordered by Indian police for unexpected foreign national deaths. Standard procedure.
Police NOC required before body can be released. Timeline depends on police district.
India's size and state variation mean timelines vary significantly by location.
Immediate embalming is critical given climate. Arrange on day of death.
Deaths in remote areas (Rajasthan, Himalayan regions, Goa beaches) add transfer time.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Indian documentation must be in certified English. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed to funeral arrangements. Deaths involving an overseas investigation may require coroner review.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British High Commission in New Delhi and consulates in Mumbai, Chennai, Kolkata, Hyderabad, Ahmedabad, and Bengaluru can register the death and advise. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: India to UK Repatriation: Guidance for British Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in India. Repatriation to the UK typically takes 4-6 weeks. Complex documentation and post-mortem routinely ordered. We guide every step. Contact us 24/7.

destinations.name: Ireland
special_rules: Irish Embassy in New Delhi handles consular matters.
Police NOC and post-mortem requirements apply identically to Irish repatriations.
Large Indian-Irish diaspora means this is an established corridor.
destinations.dest_reception: The Irish funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Indian documentation must be in certified English. The Coroner is notified.
destinations.dest_consular_note: Department of Foreign Affairs emergency line: +353 1 408 2000. The Irish Embassy in New Delhi can register the death and advise.
destinations.seo_title: India to Ireland: Funeral Repatriation Guidance
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in India and needs to come home to Ireland. Repatriation takes 4-6 weeks. Post-mortem and police NOC explained. Contact us 24/7.
